extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won the Nancy sprint following the wheel of teammate ALAPHILIPPE Julian, who unusually led out from yellow jersey with 800m remaining.
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE in yellow personally led out VIVIANI from 800m, unprecedented domestique work revealing Deceuninck's tactical desperation post-TTT disappointment.
EWAN Caleb boxed on barriers after positioning third wheel, demonstrating positional risk of following leadout trains on narrow finishes.
Team INEOS moved forward 10km from finish for safety not victory, signaling GC-only focus frees sprint chaos for others.
JANSE VAN RENSBURG Reinardt led massive acceleration through tunnel creating selection, showing Bahrain's COLBRELLI leadout timing starts earlier than rivals.

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: First Tour stage win since 2014 return, capitalized perfectly on yellow jersey leadout sacrifice
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Willingness to perform leadout work from yellow demonstrates team-first mentality despite individual GC position
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: Boxed against barriers despite holding third wheel position, suggesting sprint positioning skills lag bike speed
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: neutral: Team Jumbo-Visma committed full leadout through VAN AERT but result not mentioned, implying top-5 not podium
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Positioned behind EWAN's wheel but no result mentioned, suggesting tactical following rather than independent sprint initiation
rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: neutral: Bahrain committed JANSE VAN RENSBURG to early acceleration but COLBRELLI dropped before finale, mistimed effort
